intro: You're building a RecyclerView list backed by a real XML item layout, using the actual androidx.recyclerview APIs end to end: an adapter and ViewHolder, a DiffUtil.ItemCallback, a payload-driven partial rebind, stable ids, a LayoutManager choice, and view recycling. Each task has a failing test to turn green, and you can run the tests from the gutter in Android Studio as you go.

tasks.title: PersonAdapter - inflate and bind a real XML row
tasks.detail: Implement PersonAdapter.onCreateViewHolder to inflate R.layout.item_person and wrap it in a PersonViewHolder, and onBindViewHolder to push items[position]'s name and age into that ViewHolder's TextViews.
tasks.hint: onCreateViewHolder: LayoutInflater.from(parent.context).inflate(R.layout.item_person, parent, false), then PersonViewHolder(view). onBindViewHolder: holder.nameText.text = ...; holder.ageText.text = ....
tasks.verify: T1PersonAdapterTest is green: a laid-out RecyclerView shows the right name and age text for each row.

tasks.title: DiffUtil.ItemCallback - same item vs same content
tasks.detail: Implement PersonDiffCallback.areItemsTheSame (are these two Persons the same real-world row?) and areContentsTheSame (has anything visible actually changed?).
tasks.hint: areItemsTheSame compares oldItem.id == newItem.id. areContentsTheSame compares full equality, oldItem == newItem, since Person is a data class.
tasks.verify: T2DiffCallbackTest is green: reordering rows with unchanged content fires exactly one move and no changes, and editing a field in place fires exactly one change and no moves.

tasks.title: getChangePayload - partial rebind
tasks.detail: Implement PersonDiffCallback.getChangePayload to describe what changed (here, whether age changed), and PartialRebindAdapter's 3-arg onBindViewHolder(holder, position, payloads) to use that payload to patch just holder.ageText instead of doing a full rebind.
tasks.hint: getChangePayload returns "age" (or any non-null marker) when oldItem.age != newItem.age, else null. The 3-arg onBindViewHolder should check payloads.isEmpty(): if empty, fall back to the full 2-arg onBindViewHolder; if not, only touch holder.ageText.
tasks.verify: T3PartialRebindTest is green: after an age-only edit, ageText shows the new value, a partial rebind ran, and the full-bind count didn't increase for that row.

tasks.title: Stable ids - why an unstable id breaks move tracking
tasks.detail: Implement PersonListAdapter.getItemId to return a real persistent identity (getItem(position).id). setHasStableIds(true) is already wired up; the bug this task guards against is returning something derived from position instead of identity, which would make two different rows in the same slot look like the 'same' row.
tasks.verify: T4StableIdsTest is green: after a row moves to a new position, RecyclerView's ViewHolder for that row's id is the exact same instance as before the move, not a fresh one.

tasks.title: LayoutManager choice
tasks.detail: Implement createLayoutManager(context, spanCount) to return a GridLayoutManager(context, spanCount) when spanCount > 1, or a plain vertical LinearLayoutManager(context) otherwise.
tasks.verify: T5LayoutManagerTest is green: with spanCount 2 items lay out two-per-row in a grid, and with spanCount 1 they stack in a single column.

tasks.title: View recycling - binding must be idempotent
tasks.detail: Implement RecyclableAdapter.onBindViewHolder to bind name and age, and set badgeText's visibility (VISIBLE or INVISIBLE) from items[position].isNew in BOTH directions on every call, so a recycled ViewHolder never keeps a stale badge from whatever row it displayed before.
tasks.hint: Don't only set badgeText visible when isNew is true - also explicitly set it INVISIBLE when isNew is false, since the ViewHolder instance is reused and may already be VISIBLE from an earlier row.
tasks.verify: T6RecyclingTest is green: scrolling far past the visible viewport shows far fewer ViewHolder creations than there are items (proving reuse), and no visible row shows a badge it shouldn't.
